titleStmt.title: 292. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to President ) to President Johnson

div.head: 292. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to President Johnson Source: Johnson Library, National Security File, Memos to the President, Rostow, Vol. 105, Box 42R. Top Secret; Sensitive; Literally Eyes Only.
div.p: Herewith an initial comment on Dobrynin’s position at the lunch he initiated.No other record of this luncheon has been found.

list.item: Moscow is clearly ready to go—and eager—if you can work it out.

Their reasons are quite similar to our own: to create a good backdrop for the NPT in January; to keep the momentum of the work on missiles going into the next Administration; and, therefore, to avoid a long delay in both the NPT and the missile affairs.
The heart of the matter, then, is not so much in the refinement of the principles, but in persuading Nixon that this is the right course for him, for the President, and for the nation.

signed.note: Printed from a copy that bears this typed signature.
